metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from Alice's hand. The glass fell to the floor. The glass broke into many pieces. Alice swept them up carefully.
The farmer planted wheat seeds in the brown soil. Rain soaked the brown soil for three full days. The wheat seeds sprouted into small green shoots. The farmer harvested the wheat late in the autumn.

The painter squeezed blue paint onto a wooden palette. The painter brushed the blue paint across the wide canvas. The blue paint dried slowly in the warm studio air. The painter hung the finished canvas on the gallery wall.

The dog dug a deep hole near the old oak tree. The dog dropped a large bone into the deep hole. The dog pushed loose dirt over the large bone with both paws. The dog trotted away from the old oak tree quietly.

The baker mixed flour and butter in a large steel bowl. The baker rolled the dough flat on the wooden counter. The baker placed the flat dough into the hot brick oven. The baker pulled a golden brown pie from the hot brick oven.

The child stacked wooden blocks into a tall tower carefully. The cat bumped the tall tower with a soft gray paw. The wooden blocks tumbled down onto the living room carpet. The child picked up the wooden blocks from the living room carpet.

The fisherman cast a long line into the calm blue lake. A large trout grabbed the hook on the long line. The fisherman reeled the large trout toward the wooden dock slowly. The fisherman placed the large trout into a metal bucket.

The teacher wrote a difficult math problem on the chalkboard. The student studied the difficult math problem for several quiet minutes. The student wrote an answer below the difficult math problem carefully. The teacher marked the answer correct with a piece of red chalk.

The mechanic opened the hood of the old blue truck. The mechanic found a cracked rubber hose inside the engine compartment. The mechanic replaced the cracked rubber hose with a brand new hose. The mechanic closed the hood of the old blue truck firmly.

The squirrel found an acorn beneath the tall oak tree. The squirrel carried the acorn across the green lawn quickly. The squirrel buried the acorn in the soft garden soil carefully. The squirrel patted the soft garden soil down with small brown paws.

The librarian noticed a torn page in the old leather book. The librarian carefully removed the torn page from the old leather book. The librarian taped the torn page back together at the front desk. The librarian returned the old leather book to the wooden shelf.

The boy flew the red kite high above the sandy beach. The string of the red kite snapped in a strong gust. The red kite drifted away over the dark ocean waves slowly. The boy watched the red kite disappear beyond the distant gray horizon.

The nurse cleaned the small wound on the patient's left arm. The nurse applied white bandages to the small wound with great care. The patient rested in the quiet hospital bed for two full hours. The nurse checked the white bandages on the patient's left arm again.

The storm knocked down a large tree across the narrow road. The large tree blocked all traffic on the narrow road completely. The road crew cut the large tree into several smaller sections. The road crew cleared the smaller sections off the narrow road quickly.

The chef chopped the fresh onion into very small pieces carefully. The chef added the fresh onion to the hot cast iron pan. The fresh onion sizzled and turned golden brown in the hot oil. The chef stirred the golden brown onion with a long wooden spoon.

The spider spun a delicate web between two tall garden posts. A small fly flew directly into the delicate web at dawn. The small fly struggled against the sticky silk threads of the web. The spider wrapped the small fly in fresh white silk threads tightly.

The mail carrier placed a brown package on the front porch steps. The family dog noticed the brown package through the glass screen door. The family dog barked loudly at the brown package for five minutes. The homeowner opened the front door and picked up the brown package.

The pianist sat down at the grand piano on the bright stage. The pianist played a slow melody in a dark minor key. The audience listened to the slow melody in complete silent stillness. The audience stood up and clapped for the pianist with great enthusiasm.

The mountaineer attached a strong rope to the rocky cliff face. The mountaineer climbed the rocky cliff face for two full hours steadily. The mountaineer reached the snowy peak just before the cold sunset arrived. The mountaineer planted a small red flag on the snowy peak proudly.

The gardener dug a shallow trench along the wooden garden fence. The gardener placed tulip bulbs into the shallow trench one by one. The gardener covered the tulip bulbs with dark rich soil gently. The tulip bulbs bloomed into bright red flowers in early spring.

The photographer set up a tripod near the edge of the canyon. The photographer adjusted the camera lens for a wide panoramic shot. The sun dropped below the purple mountain ridge in the west slowly. The photographer captured the golden sunset over the deep red canyon.

The beekeeper opened the white wooden hive in the apple orchard. The beekeeper pulled out a heavy frame dripping with fresh golden honey. The beekeeper scraped the fresh golden honey into a clean glass jar. The beekeeper sealed the clean glass jar with a metal lid tightly.

The carpenter measured a long oak board with a yellow tape measure. The carpenter cut the long oak board with a sharp circular saw. The carpenter sanded the cut oak board until the surface felt smooth. The carpenter nailed the smooth oak board to the cabin wall frame.

The student opened a thick textbook to the first chapter nervously. The student read the first chapter at the quiet library desk all evening. The student wrote detailed notes about the first chapter in a blue notebook. The student closed the thick textbook and left the quiet library desk.

The potter shaped wet clay into a tall vase on the spinning wheel. The potter placed the tall vase into the hot kiln overnight carefully. The hot kiln hardened the tall vase into solid white ceramic material. The potter painted the tall vase with bright green and blue glaze.

The pilot checked all the instruments in the small cockpit before departure. The pilot taxied the single-engine plane down the long concrete runway slowly. The single-engine plane lifted off the long concrete runway into the gray sky. The pilot flew the single-engine plane above the thick white clouds.

The detective found a muddy footprint near the broken basement window. The detective measured the muddy footprint with a small metal ruler carefully. The detective photographed the muddy footprint from three different angles with a camera. The detective placed yellow evidence tape around the broken basement window perimeter.

The child dropped a large stone into the still pond at noon. The large stone sank quickly to the muddy bottom of the still pond. Circular ripples spread outward across the surface of the still pond. The circular ripples reached the grassy bank on the far side.

The waiter carried a tray of hot soup bowls to the corner table. The waiter placed the hot soup bowls in front of each dinner guest. The dinner guests tasted the hot soup with small silver spoons eagerly. The dinner guests complimented the waiter on the delicious hot soup warmly.

The lumberjack swung a heavy axe into the base of the tall pine. The tall pine cracked loudly and leaned toward the open clearing slowly. The tall pine crashed onto the ground of the open clearing with force. The lumberjack trimmed the branches off the fallen tall pine methodically.

The scientist placed a glass slide under the powerful laboratory microscope. The scientist adjusted the focus knob on the powerful laboratory microscope slowly. A cluster of green cells appeared clearly on the glass slide surface. The scientist recorded detailed observations about the green cells in a leather notebook.

The blacksmith heated a thick iron bar in the blazing hot forge. The thick iron bar glowed bright orange in the intense heat. The blacksmith hammered the glowing iron bar into a curved horseshoe shape. The blacksmith cooled the curved horseshoe in a wooden bucket of cold water.

The cat crept slowly toward a small brown bird on the garden wall. The small brown bird spotted the cat and spread two narrow wings quickly. The small brown bird flew off the garden wall into the tall elm tree. The cat sat beneath the tall elm tree and watched the branches.

The plumber discovered a leaking pipe beneath the old kitchen sink. The plumber turned off the main water valve in the dark basement. The plumber removed the leaking pipe from beneath the old kitchen sink carefully. The plumber installed a new copper pipe beneath the old kitchen sink.

The hiker followed a narrow dirt trail through the dense pine forest. The narrow dirt trail led the hiker to a clear mountain stream. The hiker filled a metal water bottle from the clear mountain stream. The hiker rested on a flat gray rock beside the clear mountain stream.

The child built a sandcastle near the edge of the ocean water. A large wave rolled up the sandy shore toward the sandcastle quickly. The large wave washed over the sandcastle and flattened the sandy walls. The child started building a new sandcastle farther from the ocean water.

The electrician turned off the power at the main breaker panel. The electrician removed the old light switch from the bedroom wall carefully. The electrician connected new wires to a modern light switch at the wall. The electrician turned the power back on at the main breaker panel.

The mother poured pancake batter onto the hot griddle in the kitchen. The pancake batter bubbled and browned on the hot griddle surface slowly. The mother flipped the pancake over with a flat metal spatula smoothly. The mother slid the golden pancake onto a warm white ceramic plate.

The archaeologist brushed sand away from a clay pot in the dry trench. The archaeologist lifted the clay pot out of the dry trench very carefully. The archaeologist wrapped the clay pot in soft cotton cloth for protection. The archaeologist carried the wrapped clay pot to the research tent nearby.

The train conductor blew a loud whistle at the old stone station. The passengers boarded the long gray train through the open side doors. The train conductor closed the open side doors of the long gray train. The long gray train pulled away from the old stone station slowly.

The tailor measured the customer's arms and shoulders with a cloth tape. The tailor cut dark blue fabric according to the recorded measurements precisely. The tailor sewed the dark blue fabric into a fitted jacket carefully. The customer tried on the fitted jacket in front of the tall mirror.

The window washer climbed a tall aluminum ladder to the third floor. The window washer sprayed soapy water on the dirty office window glass. The window washer wiped the dirty office window glass with a rubber squeegee. The window washer climbed down the tall aluminum ladder to the ground.

The zookeeper unlocked the heavy iron gate of the lion enclosure slowly. The zookeeper placed a large metal tray of raw meat inside the enclosure. The old male lion walked toward the large metal tray of raw meat. The zookeeper locked the heavy iron gate of the lion enclosure again.

The jeweler examined the rough diamond under a bright magnifying lamp closely. The jeweler cut the rough diamond along the natural crystal grain lines. The jeweler polished every facet of the cut diamond with a fine cloth. The cut diamond sparkled under the bright magnifying lamp with intense light.

The sailor noticed dark storm clouds forming on the eastern horizon ahead. The sailor lowered the white canvas sails on the wooden mast quickly. The dark storm clouds brought heavy rain and strong winds to the sea. The sailor anchored the boat in a sheltered cove near the rocky shore.

The locksmith examined the jammed front door lock of the old brick house. The locksmith inserted a thin metal pick into the jammed front door lock. The locksmith turned the thin metal pick and released the stuck mechanism inside. The front door of the old brick house swung open without resistance.

The florist trimmed the long stems of the fresh red roses with scissors. The florist arranged the fresh red roses in a tall crystal vase carefully. The florist added green fern leaves around the fresh red roses for contrast. The florist wrapped the tall crystal vase in clear cellophane for delivery.

The bricklayer spread wet cement along the top of the brick wall. The bricklayer pressed a new red brick into the wet cement firmly. The bricklayer checked the level of the new red brick with a metal tool. The bricklayer scraped excess wet cement off the sides of the brick wall.

The magician placed a white rabbit inside the tall black top hat. The magician tapped the tall black top hat three times with a wand. The magician turned the tall black top hat upside down on the table. The white rabbit had vanished from inside the tall black top hat completely.

The sculptor carved a block of white marble with a sharp steel chisel. The sculptor shaped the block of white marble into a human figure slowly. The sculptor smoothed the surface of the human figure with fine sandpaper. The sculptor displayed the finished human figure in the town museum lobby.

The ranger spotted a thin column of gray smoke from the fire tower. The ranger radioed the base camp about the thin column of gray smoke. The base camp sent a fire crew to the source of the gray smoke. The fire crew extinguished a small brush fire before the flames spread.

The boy tossed a flat stone across the surface of the quiet river. The flat stone skipped four times across the surface of the quiet river. The flat stone lost speed and sank into the deep quiet river water. The boy picked up another flat stone from the muddy river bank.

The diver jumped off the high board into the deep swimming pool below. The diver sliced through the blue water of the deep swimming pool cleanly. The diver touched the tiled bottom of the deep swimming pool with both hands. The diver kicked upward and surfaced at the top of the deep pool.

The cobbler removed the worn leather sole from the old brown boot carefully. The cobbler glued a new leather sole onto the bottom of the old brown boot. The cobbler stitched the new leather sole to the old brown boot tightly. The cobbler polished the old brown boot until the leather shone brightly.

The typist loaded a blank sheet of paper into the old typewriter carefully. The typist pressed the metal keys of the old typewriter with quick steady fingers. The old typewriter stamped black ink letters onto the blank sheet of paper. The typist pulled the finished sheet of paper from the old typewriter.

The geologist cracked open a large gray rock with a steel hammer blow. The large gray rock split in two and revealed purple crystals inside. The geologist examined the purple crystals with a small handheld magnifying glass closely. The geologist placed the purple crystals into a padded sample collection box.

The weaver stretched long cotton threads across the wide wooden loom frame. The weaver passed a wooden shuttle through the long cotton threads back and forth. The long cotton threads formed a tight colorful pattern on the wide loom. The weaver cut the finished cloth from the wide wooden loom frame.

The dentist examined the patient's teeth under a bright overhead examination lamp. The dentist found a small cavity in the patient's lower left molar tooth. The dentist drilled the small cavity out of the patient's lower left molar. The dentist filled the drilled molar with a silver amalgam material.

The farmer's border collie spotted a stray sheep near the far stone wall. The border collie ran across the green pasture toward the stray sheep quickly. The border collie herded the stray sheep back toward the rest of the flock. The stray sheep rejoined the rest of the flock near the red barn.

The surfer paddled a long white surfboard out past the breaking waves. A tall wave formed behind the surfer near the outer reef line. The surfer stood up on the long white surfboard and rode the tall wave. The tall wave carried the surfer and the long white surfboard toward shore.

The watchmaker removed the tiny gears from the broken gold pocket watch. The watchmaker cleaned the tiny gears in a small dish of clear solution. The watchmaker placed the clean tiny gears back into the gold pocket watch. The gold pocket watch ticked steadily once the watchmaker wound the small crown.

The owl perched silently on a high branch of the dead elm tree. The owl spotted a field mouse moving through the dark meadow grass below. The owl swooped down from the dead elm tree toward the field mouse. The owl caught the field mouse in two sharp talons and flew away.

The glassblower gathered molten glass on the end of a long metal pipe. The glassblower blew air through the long metal pipe into the molten glass. The molten glass expanded into a round hollow bubble shape on the pipe. The glassblower shaped the round bubble into a delicate red glass bowl.

The truck driver loaded heavy wooden crates onto the flatbed truck at dawn. The truck driver drove the flatbed truck down the long dusty highway for hours. The truck driver arrived at the warehouse on the far side of town. The truck driver unloaded the heavy wooden crates at the warehouse loading dock.

The barber wrapped a white cloth around the customer's neck and shoulders. The barber sprayed warm water on the customer's thick dark hair with a bottle. The barber cut the customer's thick dark hair with sharp silver scissors. The barber removed the white cloth and brushed loose hair off the customer's shoulders.

The astronomer pointed the large telescope toward the northern sky after sunset. The astronomer adjusted the focus dial on the large telescope very slowly. A distant cluster of faint stars appeared in the eyepiece of the large telescope. The astronomer sketched the faint star cluster in a hardcover observation journal.

The mason dug a deep trench for the foundation of the new garden wall. The mason poured wet concrete into the deep trench up to ground level. The wet concrete hardened in the deep trench over the next two full days. The mason laid the first row of gray stone blocks on the concrete.

The young eagle spread two wide brown wings at the edge of the cliff. The young eagle jumped off the edge of the cliff into the open air. The young eagle caught a warm updraft and rose above the deep green valley. The young eagle circled high above the deep green valley three full times.

The chemist poured a clear liquid into a glass beaker on the lab bench. The chemist added three drops of blue indicator solution to the glass beaker. The clear liquid in the glass beaker turned a deep shade of purple instantly. The chemist recorded the color change result in a laboratory notebook carefully.

The street musician set up a wooden guitar case on the busy sidewalk. The street musician played a lively folk song on an old acoustic guitar. Passing pedestrians stopped and listened to the lively folk song with clear enjoyment. Passing pedestrians dropped coins and bills into the open wooden guitar case generously.

The potter's apprentice wedged a lump of wet gray clay on the wooden table. The potter's apprentice placed the lump of wet gray clay on the spinning wheel. The potter's apprentice shaped the wet gray clay into a wide shallow bowl slowly. The potter's apprentice set the wide shallow bowl aside on a drying rack.

The janitor mopped the long marble hallway of the office building after midnight. The janitor noticed a dark stain near the elevator doors on the marble floor. The janitor scrubbed the dark stain with a stiff brush and strong cleaning solution. The dark stain faded from the marble floor after several minutes of scrubbing.

The rancher noticed a broken section in the wooden pasture fence at dawn. The rancher's cattle wandered through the broken section into the neighbor's cornfield overnight. The rancher herded the cattle back through the broken section of the wooden fence. The rancher repaired the broken section of the wooden pasture fence with new boards.

The postal clerk stamped each white envelope with a round red postmark at the counter. The postal clerk sorted the stamped white envelopes into labeled bins by zip code. The postal clerk loaded the labeled bins onto a rolling metal cart in the back room. The postal clerk pushed the rolling metal cart to the outgoing mail truck outside.

The swimmer dove into the cold lake water from the end of the wooden dock. The swimmer kicked through the cold lake water toward the small rocky island ahead. The swimmer reached the small rocky island and climbed onto the warm gray rocks. The swimmer rested on the warm gray rocks of the small rocky island.

The electrician climbed the tall wooden utility pole with steel climbing spikes on. The electrician reached the damaged power line at the top of the utility pole. The electrician spliced the damaged power line with fresh copper wire and metal connectors. The lights in the nearby houses turned back on after the repair.

The bird built a small nest of dry twigs in the crook of the maple tree. The bird laid four tiny blue eggs inside the small nest of dry twigs. The bird sat on the four tiny blue eggs for two full weeks patiently. The four tiny blue eggs cracked open and four chicks emerged inside the nest.

The surgeon scrubbed both hands thoroughly at the stainless steel wash station before surgery. The surgeon made a small precise incision on the patient's right knee with a scalpel. The surgeon repaired the torn ligament inside the patient's right knee with sutures. The surgeon closed the small incision on the patient's right knee with stitches.

The miner swung a heavy pickaxe against the dark rock wall underground. The heavy pickaxe broke through the dark rock wall and revealed a vein of ore. The miner chipped away at the vein of ore with a smaller pointed tool. The miner loaded chunks of the ore into a metal cart on the rails.

The mail carrier noticed a large puddle blocking the path to the front door. The mail carrier stepped around the large puddle onto the dry garden stones carefully. The mail carrier placed the stack of letters inside the black metal mailbox. The mail carrier walked back around the large puddle to the delivery truck.

The sculptor mixed plaster powder and water in a wide plastic bucket steadily. The sculptor poured the wet plaster mixture into a rectangular silicone mold on the table. The wet plaster mixture hardened inside the rectangular silicone mold over several hours. The sculptor peeled the rectangular silicone mold away from the solid plaster block.

The camp counselor gathered dry sticks and crumpled newspaper in a stone fire ring. The camp counselor struck a match and lit the crumpled newspaper in the stone fire ring. The crumpled newspaper caught fire and ignited the dry sticks above with orange flames. The camp counselor added larger logs to the growing fire in the stone ring.

The crane operator positioned the large steel crane above the construction site foundation. The crane operator attached thick cables to a heavy concrete beam on the ground. The crane operator lifted the heavy concrete beam high above the construction site slowly. The crane operator lowered the heavy concrete beam onto the top of the steel support columns.

The knitter wound a ball of soft blue yarn from a fresh skein at home. The knitter cast forty stitches onto a pair of long bamboo knitting needles. The knitter worked each row of soft blue yarn into a ribbed scarf pattern. The knitter bound off the last row and trimmed the loose yarn ends.

The park ranger found a fallen log blocking the main hiking trail at sunrise. The park ranger sawed the fallen log into two shorter pieces with a handsaw. The park ranger rolled the two shorter pieces off the main hiking trail carefully. The park ranger cleared the remaining bark and debris off the main hiking trail.

The archaeologist discovered a buried clay tablet at the bottom of the excavation pit. The archaeologist brushed dry sand off the surface of the buried clay tablet. The archaeologist read ancient symbols carved into the surface of the clay tablet closely. The archaeologist placed the clay tablet into a cushioned wooden storage crate.

The goalkeeper spotted the forward running toward the goal with the soccer ball. The forward kicked the soccer ball hard toward the lower left corner of the goal. The goalkeeper dove to the left and blocked the soccer ball with two gloved hands. The soccer ball bounced away from the goalkeeper and rolled out of bounds.

The woodpecker tapped the bark of the tall dead pine tree with a sharp beak. The woodpecker drilled a small round hole into the bark of the tall dead pine. The woodpecker pulled a fat white grub out of the small round hole. The woodpecker swallowed the fat white grub and flew to the next dead tree.

The printer loaded a fresh stack of white paper into the large office printer tray. The office worker pressed the green start button on the front of the large printer. The large office printer pulled sheets from the paper tray and printed black text. The office worker collected the printed pages from the output tray of the printer.

The wind pushed dark rain clouds over the dry wheat field from the west. The dark rain clouds released heavy rainfall onto the dry wheat field below steadily. The dry wheat field absorbed the heavy rainfall into the cracked brown soil quickly. The wheat stalks in the field stood taller after the heavy rainfall ended.

The violinist opened a black leather case and removed the old wooden violin carefully. The violinist rosined the horsehair bow with a small amber block of rosin slowly. The violinist placed the old wooden violin under a raised chin and drew the bow. A rich warm note filled the quiet concert hall from the old wooden violin.

The stone skipped across the frozen surface of the small winter pond at dusk. The frozen surface of the small winter pond cracked in a thin jagged line. The thin jagged crack spread outward from the point of impact across the ice. The stone slid along the cracked ice to the far shore of the pond.

The tailor noticed a loose button on the front of the navy wool coat. The tailor threaded a steel needle with strong dark navy thread at the workbench. The tailor sewed the loose button back onto the front of the navy wool coat. The tailor knotted the dark navy thread and cut the excess with small scissors.

The chef placed a raw salmon fillet onto the hot grill over glowing charcoal. The raw salmon fillet sizzled and cooked on the hot grill for four minutes. The chef flipped the salmon fillet over with a wide metal spatula carefully. The chef removed the cooked salmon fillet from the hot grill onto a plate.

The fox crept through the tall brown grass toward the hen house at dusk. The fox squeezed through a narrow gap in the old wooden hen house fence. The rooster inside the hen house spotted the fox and crowed a loud alarm. The fox retreated through the narrow gap in the old wooden fence quickly.

The roofer carried a bundle of brown shingles up the tall extension ladder. The roofer removed the old damaged shingles from the peak of the roof first. The roofer nailed the new brown shingles onto the exposed wooden roof deck firmly. The roofer climbed down the tall extension ladder after finishing the roof repair.

The cashier scanned each grocery item across the red laser reader at the register. The cashier placed the scanned grocery items into brown paper bags on the counter. The cashier read the total price amount on the register screen to the customer. The customer handed the cashier a stack of paper bills to pay the total.

The river rose three feet overnight after heavy rain fell in the upper mountains. The rising river water spilled over the low dirt banks near the old wooden bridge. The rising river water flooded the flat meadow on the east side of the bridge. The town workers stacked heavy sandbags along the low dirt banks for protection.
noncausal_texts: A violin is played with a bow. A flute is played by blowing air. A drum is played by striking it. A harp is played by plucking strings.

Copper conducted electricity well and had a reddish surface color. Lead was very dense and resisted corrosion from most common acids. Tin melted at a low temperature and coated other metals easily. Zinc protected iron from rust when applied as a thin coating.

The Sahara Desert covered a vast area of northern Africa entirely. The Gobi Desert stretched across parts of Mongolia and northern China. The Atacama Desert sat along the western coast of South America. The Kalahari Desert spread across several countries in southern Africa.

A maple tree produced winged seeds that spun while falling to the ground. A pine tree held small seeds inside tough woody cones on the branches. An oak tree dropped rounded acorns that squirrels buried in the soil. A willow tree released tiny fluffy seeds that drifted on the wind.

Table salt was a compound of sodium and chlorine atoms bonded together. Baking soda contained sodium and hydrogen and carbon and oxygen atoms combined. Vinegar consisted mostly of water mixed with a small amount of acetic acid. Sugar was a compound of carbon and hydrogen and oxygen atoms arranged in rings.

The Amazon River flowed eastward through dense tropical rainforest to the Atlantic Ocean. The Danube River passed through ten different European countries on the way to the sea. The Nile River ran northward through arid desert land to the Mediterranean Sea. The Mississippi River drained a vast basin in the central part of North America.

Granite was an igneous rock formed from cooled magma deep underground over time. Sandstone was a sedimentary rock made of compressed sand grains cemented together naturally. Marble was a metamorphic rock created when limestone experienced intense heat and pressure. Obsidian was a volcanic glass formed when lava cooled extremely rapidly at the surface.

A camel stored fat in a large hump on the back for desert travel. A polar bear had thick white fur for warmth in frozen arctic regions. A dolphin used a blowhole on top of the head for breathing air. A chameleon changed skin color to match the surrounding environment for protection.

Brazil produced large quantities of coffee beans on hillside plantations every year. India grew vast amounts of tea leaves on terraced mountain gardens in the east. Mexico cultivated many varieties of hot chili peppers across the southern states. Japan harvested edible seaweed from coastal ocean waters for use in traditional dishes.

Mercury orbited closest to the sun of all the planets in the solar system. Jupiter contained more mass than all the other planets in the solar system combined. Saturn displayed a wide system of bright rings made of ice and rock particles. Neptune existed as the most distant major planet from the sun in the system.

A sonnet contained fourteen lines of verse arranged in a specific rhyme scheme. A haiku consisted of three lines with five and seven and five syllables each. A limerick followed a pattern of five lines with an AABBA rhyme structure. A ballad told a story through simple stanzas with a repeating refrain pattern.

Cotton grew in warm climates and produced soft white fiber for clothing material. Silk came from the cocoons of silkworm larvae raised on mulberry tree leaves. Wool was shorn from the coats of sheep once or twice each calendar year. Linen was woven from the fibers of the flax plant grown in cool regions.

A barometer measured atmospheric air pressure to help predict incoming weather patterns. A thermometer measured temperature using the expansion of liquid inside a sealed glass tube. A hygrometer measured the amount of moisture present in the surrounding air. An anemometer measured the speed of wind using spinning cups on a vertical axis.

The Colosseum in Rome seated approximately fifty thousand spectators in tiered stone rows. The Parthenon in Athens featured tall marble columns arranged in a rectangular layout. The Great Wall of China extended thousands of miles across mountainous northern terrain. The Taj Mahal in India displayed intricate white marble carvings on every exterior surface.

A starfish moved along the ocean floor using hundreds of tiny tube feet. A jellyfish drifted through open water using a pulsing bell-shaped translucent body. An octopus propelled forward by pushing water out through a narrow muscular siphon. A sea urchin crawled on rocky surfaces using long sharp spines and tube feet.

Iron rusted when exposed to moisture and oxygen in the open air. Gold resisted tarnishing and remained shiny even after centuries of open exposure. Aluminum formed a thin protective oxide layer when exposed to the open air. Silver darkened over time when exposed to sulfur compounds present in the air.

A compass needle pointed toward magnetic north due to the earth's magnetic field. A sundial cast a shadow that indicated the approximate time of day outdoors. A sextant measured the angle between a celestial body and the visible horizon line. An hourglass tracked the passage of time using sand flowing through a narrow opening.

The violin section sat at the front left of a standard symphony orchestra. The brass section occupied the back rows of a standard symphony orchestra arrangement. The percussion section was positioned at the rear of a standard symphony orchestra. The woodwind section sat in the center rows of a standard symphony orchestra.

A hexagon had six equal sides and six interior angles of equal measure. A pentagon had five equal sides and five interior angles of equal measure. An octagon had eight equal sides and eight interior angles of equal measure. A decagon had ten equal sides and ten interior angles of equal measure.

Basalt was a dark fine-grained volcanic rock found on ocean floor surfaces worldwide. Quartz was a hard crystalline mineral found in many types of rock formations. Feldspar made up a large percentage of the earth's continental crust by volume. Mica split into thin shiny flexible sheets along flat cleavage planes in the rock.

A crocodile lived in tropical freshwater rivers and lakes near muddy banks. A komodo dragon inhabited dry grassland and forest on a few Indonesian islands. A sea turtle swam across entire ocean basins during long seasonal migrations each year. A tortoise roamed dry scrubland and ate tough plants and cactus pads on land.

Red light had the longest wavelength of all colors in the visible spectrum. Blue light scattered more in the atmosphere than other visible light colors did. Green light was absorbed by chlorophyll in plant leaves during the photosynthesis process. Yellow light fell in the middle range of the visible electromagnetic light spectrum.

An abacus was used for arithmetic calculations in ancient China and Rome. A slide rule performed multiplication and division using logarithmic scales on sliding bars. A mechanical calculator used interlocking metal gears to perform addition and subtraction operations. A punch card stored data as patterns of holes in stiff rectangular paper cards.

Cinnamon came from the inner bark of tropical trees in the laurel family. Black pepper grew as small berries on climbing vines in tropical Asian regions. Saffron was harvested from the dried stigmas of the crocus flower by hand. Vanilla was extracted from the seed pods of a tropical climbing orchid plant.

A cathedral typically featured a long central nave flanked by side aisles and columns. A mosque typically featured a large open prayer hall facing the direction of Mecca. A pagoda typically featured multiple tiered roofs stacked above a central tower structure. A synagogue typically featured a raised platform for reading sacred texts in the center.

A penguin was a flightless bird that swam underwater using flipper-shaped wings for propulsion. An ostrich was a flightless bird that ran at high speeds across open grassland. A kiwi was a flightless bird that foraged at night using a long sensitive beak. An emu was a flightless bird that roamed the dry interior of the Australian continent.

Wheat was a staple grain crop grown in temperate regions across many different continents. Rice was a staple grain crop grown in flooded paddy fields across tropical Asia. Corn was a staple grain crop first domesticated in the region of central Mexico. Millet was a staple grain crop grown in dry arid regions of Africa and Asia.

A tsunami was caused by underwater earthquakes or volcanic eruptions on the ocean floor. A tornado formed from rotating air columns inside severe thunderstorm cloud systems on land. A blizzard combined heavy snowfall with strong sustained winds and very low air temperatures. A monsoon brought seasonal heavy rainfall driven by shifting wind patterns over large regions.

A saxophone was invented in Belgium in the eighteen forties by a musical instrument maker. A piano was developed in Italy around the year seventeen hundred by a harpsichord builder. A harmonica was first mass-produced in Germany in the early eighteen hundreds by craftsmen. A banjo was developed in the Caribbean region from instruments brought from West Africa.

Limestone formed from the compressed remains of ancient marine shells on the sea floor. Chalk was a soft white sedimentary rock made of microscopic plankton shells accumulated underwater. Slate was a fine-grained metamorphic rock that split easily into flat thin durable sheets. Pumice was a lightweight volcanic rock full of tiny gas bubble holes throughout.

A bald eagle built large nests in tall trees near rivers and coastal waterways. A barn owl roosted in old wooden buildings and hollow tree cavities during the day. A hummingbird hovered in front of flowers and fed on sweet plant nectar through the beak. A pelican caught fish by diving from the air and scooping water in a throat pouch.

The cello was tuned in intervals of perfect fifths from low to high strings. The guitar was tuned using a combination of perfect fourths and one major third interval. The ukulele was tuned in a re-entrant pattern with the top string at a higher pitch. The mandolin was tuned in pairs of unison strings arranged in intervals of perfect fifths.

A submarine navigated underwater using sonar to detect objects and map the ocean floor. A hot air balloon rose by heating the air inside a large fabric envelope. A helicopter lifted off the ground using rotating blades mounted on a vertical shaft above. A sailboat moved across water by catching wind in large fabric sails on tall masts.

Copper was used for electrical wiring because copper conducted electricity very efficiently in circuits. Titanium was used in aircraft frames because titanium was both strong and very lightweight. Tungsten was used in light bulb filaments because tungsten had a very high melting point. Rubber was used for vehicle tires because rubber gripped road surfaces and absorbed heavy shocks.

A glacier carved deep U-shaped valleys by slowly grinding rock surfaces over many centuries. A river carved narrow V-shaped valleys by eroding rock along a flowing water channel. An ocean wave carved sea cliffs by repeatedly crashing against exposed coastal rock formations. A wind current carved rock arches by blasting sand particles against desert stone surfaces.

An igloo was built from blocks of compacted snow arranged in a spiral dome shape. A yurt was constructed from a collapsible wooden frame covered with thick animal felt layers. A longhouse was built from wooden poles covered with sheets of bark in forested regions. An adobe house was constructed from sun-dried mud bricks stacked into thick solid walls.

A waltz was danced in three-quarter time with a strong accent on the first beat. A tango was danced with sharp staccato steps and dramatic pauses between movement phrases. A polka was danced with quick half-steps in a lively two-four time signature rhythm. A foxtrot was danced with smooth gliding steps in a slow four-four time signature.

Dutch was spoken as the primary official language in the Netherlands and in Belgium. Swahili was spoken as a common trade language across many countries in East Africa. Portuguese was spoken as the primary official language in Brazil and in Portugal. Malay was spoken as the primary official language in Malaysia and in Brunei.

A frog absorbed oxygen through moist skin as well as through simple lung structures. A fish extracted dissolved oxygen from water by passing water over thin gill membranes. An insect drew air into the body through small openings called spiracles along the sides. A bird moved air through a system of air sacs connected to rigid lightweight lungs.

Sapphire was a blue gemstone made of the mineral corundum found in metamorphic rock. Emerald was a green gemstone made of the mineral beryl colored by trace chromium atoms. Ruby was a red gemstone made of the mineral corundum colored by trace chromium atoms. Topaz was a gemstone found in many colors inside cavities of ignite rock formations.

A cactus stored water inside a thick fleshy stem protected by sharp needle spines. A baobab tree stored water inside a massive swollen trunk during the long dry season. A mangrove tree grew in salty coastal water using arching aerial root structures for support. A bamboo plant grew extremely fast and formed dense groves of hollow woody stems.

Sourdough bread was leavened using a natural culture of wild yeast and lactobacillus bacteria. Naan bread was cooked by pressing flat dough against the inside wall of a tandoor oven. Tortillas were made from ground corn or wheat flour pressed into thin flat round discs. Pita bread puffed up during baking and formed a hollow interior pocket of air.

A fresco was painted directly onto wet plaster on a wall or ceiling surface. A mosaic was assembled from small pieces of colored glass or stone set in mortar. A tapestry was woven on a large loom using colored threads to form images. An engraving was cut into a metal plate and printed with ink onto paper.

A stethoscope amplified internal body sounds through a hollow tube to the ears of a doctor. A scalpel provided a very sharp precise blade for making small surgical incisions in tissue. Surgical forceps gripped and held tissue or small objects during delicate medical procedures. A syringe delivered measured amounts of liquid medication through a hollow pointed needle tip.

A stalactite hung from the ceiling of a limestone cave and grew downward slowly. A stalagmite rose from the floor of a limestone cave and grew upward slowly. A sinkhole formed when the ground above an underground cave collapsed suddenly inward. An underground river flowed through channels dissolved in limestone rock by mildly acidic groundwater over time.

A manatee grazed on underwater sea grass in warm shallow coastal waters and rivers. A narwhal possessed a long spiral tusk that extended forward from the upper jaw area. A walrus used two large curved tusks to pull up onto floating ice platforms. A dugong fed on sea grass beds in the warm coastal waters of the Indian Ocean.

Cast iron was brittle and contained a relatively high percentage of carbon in the alloy. Stainless steel resisted rust because stainless steel contained chromium mixed into the iron alloy. Bronze was an alloy of copper and tin used for bells and statues since ancient times. Brass was an alloy of copper and zinc with a bright golden yellow appearance.

An hourglass shape was wider at the top and bottom and narrow in the center. A crescent shape curved like the lit portion of the moon in a partial phase. A spiral shape wound outward from a central point in a continuous curving path. A zigzag shape consisted of short straight lines that changed direction at sharp angles repeatedly.

Sandstone cliffs displayed horizontal bands of red and yellow and white mineral layers clearly. A geode looked like a plain round rock but contained sparkling crystals inside the hollow center. Petrified wood formed when minerals slowly replaced the original organic wood cells over many centuries. Fool's gold resembled real gold but was actually iron pyrite with a brassy color.

A hand fan was waved back and forth to create a cooling breeze in hot weather. A windmill used rotating blades to capture wind energy for grinding grain into flour. A bellows pushed air into a forge fire to increase the flame temperature dramatically. A weather vane rotated on a vertical axis to indicate the current wind direction.

Turmeric produced a bright yellow color and was used widely in South Asian cooking. Paprika produced a deep red color and was made from dried ground sweet pepper pods. Matcha produced a vivid green color and was made from finely ground tea leaves. Saffron produced a rich golden orange color and was harvested from crocus flower stigmas.

Origami was the Japanese art of folding flat paper into decorative sculptural shapes without cutting. Calligraphy was the art of writing letters and words with a brush or special pen. Pottery was the craft of shaping wet clay into vessels and firing the clay in a kiln. Weaving was the craft of interlacing threads on a loom to produce flat cloth.

A raven had a wedge-shaped tail and a thick heavy beak for eating varied foods. A crow had a fan-shaped tail and a slender beak suited for picking small items. A magpie had a very long tail and bold black and white plumage markings. A jay had a short rounded tail and bright blue feather patches on the wings.

Mercury was a liquid metal at room temperature and appeared as shiny silver droplets. Bromine was a liquid nonmetal at room temperature and appeared as a dark reddish brown fluid. Gallium melted in the warmth of a human hand just above room temperature easily. Cesium was a soft golden metal that reacted violently with water on contact.

A kayak was paddled with a double-bladed paddle from a seated position in the cockpit. A canoe was paddled with a single-bladed paddle from a kneeling or seated position inside. A gondola was propelled by a standing operator using a single long oar at the stern. A punt was moved along shallow water by pushing a long pole against the riverbed.

An artichoke was a large thistle bud eaten by removing and scraping the fleshy leaf bases. A brussels sprout was a small dense cabbage bud that grew along a tall thick stalk. A fennel bulb had a crisp white base with a mild sweet anise flavor when eaten raw. A kohlrabi was a swollen stem vegetable with a mild turnip-like taste when peeled.

A theremin was played by moving hands near two metal antennas without touching the instrument. A didgeridoo was played by vibrating the lips into a long hollow wooden tube continuously. A steel drum was played by striking tuned concave sections on a modified oil barrel surface. Bagpipes were played by squeezing air from a leather bag through multiple reed pipes.

An opal displayed shifting rainbow colors caused by light diffracting through tiny silica spheres inside. A diamond was the hardest natural mineral and was made entirely of carbon atoms bonded tightly. An amethyst was a purple variety of quartz colored by trace iron impurities in the crystal. A pearl formed inside a mollusk shell around an irritant grain coated with nacre layers.

A platypus detected prey underwater using electrical sensors located in the flat rubbery bill. An elephant detected low-frequency sounds through the ground using sensitive nerve endings in the feet. A pit viper detected warm-blooded prey using heat-sensing pits located between the eye and nostril. A shark detected faint electrical fields produced by the muscles of nearby swimming fish.

A sandbar formed from sand deposited by ocean currents in shallow water near the coastline. A delta formed where a river deposited sediment upon entering a larger body of still water. A moraine was a ridge of rocks and dirt pushed forward and left by a retreating glacier. An alluvial fan formed where a fast mountain stream spread out onto a flat plain.

A pyramid had a square base and four triangular faces that met at a single point. A cylinder had two circular flat ends connected by a single curved surface between the ends. A cone had one circular flat base and a curved surface that tapered to a point. A prism had two identical polygon faces connected by flat rectangular surfaces along the sides.

A samosa was a fried triangular pastry filled with spiced potatoes and peas from South Asia. An empanada was a baked or fried turnover filled with meat or cheese from Latin America. A pierogi was a boiled dumpling filled with potato and cheese from Eastern Europe. A spring roll was a fried wrapper filled with vegetables and meat from East Asia.

A beaver built dams across streams using branches and mud to create still water ponds. A termite built tall mound structures from chewed soil and saliva in tropical grassland areas. A weaver bird built intricate hanging nests woven from long grass strips in tall tree branches. A prairie dog built underground burrow networks with multiple tunnel entrances across open grassland areas.

Basil was a fragrant herb used commonly in Italian and Thai cooking for flavoring dishes. Cilantro was an herb with a distinctive sharp taste used in Mexican and Indian food. Rosemary was a woody herb with needle-shaped leaves used to season roasted meat and potatoes. Dill was a feathery herb used to flavor pickled cucumbers and fish dishes in many regions.

A contrabassoon played notes in the lowest register of the standard woodwind instrument family. A piccolo played notes in the highest register of the standard woodwind instrument family. An English horn played notes in the middle-to-low range of the standard woodwind instrument family. A bass clarinet played notes in the low register of the standard woodwind instrument family.

A trilobite was a marine arthropod that lived in ancient oceans during the Paleozoic era. An ammonite was a spiral-shelled mollusk that lived in oceans during the Mesozoic era widely. A mammoth was a large tusked mammal that roamed cold grasslands during the Pleistocene ice ages. A pteranodon was a large flying reptile that soared above coastal waters during the Cretaceous period.

A dhow sailed the waters of the Indian Ocean using a triangular lateen sail rig. A junk sailed the waters of the South China Sea using rectangular battened sail panels. A longship sailed the waters of the North Atlantic using a single large square sail. A felucca sailed the waters of the Nile River using a tall angled lateen sail.

A stapler fastened sheets of paper together by driving thin metal staples through the pages. A paper clip held sheets of paper together by clamping bent wire around the page edges. A rubber band held rolled papers together by stretching an elastic loop around the bundle. A binder clip held thick stacks of paper together by clamping spring-loaded metal arms.

A fez was a flat-topped red felt hat traditionally worn in Ottoman and North African regions. A beret was a soft round flat-crowned hat traditionally associated with French and Basque culture. A turban was a long cloth wrapped around the head in many South Asian and Middle Eastern cultures. A sombrero was a wide-brimmed high-crowned hat traditionally worn in Mexican and Spanish culture.

A sandpiper probed wet shoreline mud for small invertebrates with a long thin sensitive beak. A flamingo filtered tiny organisms from shallow lake water through a specialized curved beak shape. A toucan reached fruit on distant thin branches using a large lightweight colorful beak structure. A crossbill pried open conifer cone scales to extract seeds using a uniquely crossed beak tip.

Limestone was dissolved slowly by mildly acidic rainwater seeping through cracks in the rock formation. Basalt was formed from fast-cooling lava flows that spread across the earth's surface from eruptions. Gneiss was formed deep underground when existing rock was subjected to extreme heat and pressure. Conglomerate was formed from rounded pebbles and gravel cemented together by natural mineral deposits.

A metronome kept steady musical time by swinging a weighted pendulum back and forth rhythmically. A tuning fork produced a single pure tone when struck against a hard surface firmly. A pitch pipe provided reference notes for singers to match before beginning a choral performance. A music stand held printed sheet music at a comfortable reading height for seated performers.

A clam burrowed into soft sandy ocean floor sediment using a single muscular foot structure. A mussel attached to hard rocky surfaces using strong sticky threads called byssal thread fibers. An oyster cemented to submerged rocks and other shells using a natural calcium carbonate adhesive. A scallop swam short distances through water by clapping two ribbed shell valves together rapidly.

A cenote was a natural sinkhole in limestone that exposed groundwater in the Yucatan region. A fjord was a deep narrow inlet carved by glaciers along the steep coastline of Norway. An atoll was a ring-shaped coral reef enclosing a shallow tropical lagoon in the open ocean. A butte was a tall isolated flat-topped hill with steep sides found in arid landscapes.

An almanac compiled seasonal weather data and astronomical events for each calendar year in advance. An atlas contained a bound collection of geographic maps organized by region or by theme. A thesaurus listed words grouped by similar meaning to help writers find alternative word choices. An encyclopedia contained articles on a wide range of topics arranged in alphabetical order.

A chisel was used to carve and shape wood or stone by striking the handle end. A plane was used to smooth and flatten wooden surfaces by shaving thin curled layers off. An awl was used to punch small holes in leather or wood for stitching or nailing. A clamp was used to hold two pieces of material firmly together while glue dried.

Cloves were the dried flower buds of a tropical tree native to the Maluku Islands. Cardamom was a seed pod from a plant in the ginger family grown in southern India. Nutmeg was the dried seed kernel of a tropical fruit tree from the Banda Islands. Star anise was the dried fruit of an evergreen tree native to southern China.

A yak carried heavy loads at high altitudes in the mountain regions of Central Asia. A llama carried packs along narrow trails in the mountain regions of South America. A donkey carried supplies on rough terrain in dry and mountainous regions across many continents. A reindeer pulled sleds across frozen tundra in the arctic regions of northern Scandinavia.

Papyrus was made from the pressed pith of a water reed plant in ancient Egypt. Parchment was made from the stretched and dried skin of sheep or goats for writing. Vellum was made from the specially prepared skin of young calves for fine manuscript work. Rice paper was made from the pressed pith of a small tree grown in East Asia.

A sauna heated the body using dry air warmed by hot stones inside a wooden room. A hammam cleaned the body using steam and warm water inside a domed stone building. A hot spring warmed the body using naturally heated groundwater rising from deep volcanic rock layers. An onsen bathed the body in mineral-rich geothermally heated water from a natural Japanese spring.

A suspension bridge hung the roadway from thick cables attached to tall vertical tower supports. An arch bridge supported the roadway using a curved structure that transferred weight to the foundations. A drawbridge raised a section of the roadway to allow tall boats to pass through below. A pontoon bridge floated the roadway on a series of flat-bottomed boats or hollow containers.

Morse code represented letters using sequences of short and long signals called dots and dashes. Braille represented letters using patterns of raised dots arranged in a small rectangular cell shape. Semaphore represented letters using the positions of two hand-held flags at specific arm angles. Sign language represented words and concepts using hand shapes and movements and facial expressions.

A woodchuck dug deep burrow systems in open meadows and along the edges of forest clearings. A mole dug shallow tunnel networks just below the surface of soft grassy lawn areas. A badger dug large underground dens called setts with multiple chambers and entrance tunnels in hillsides. A gopher dug extensive tunnel systems in loose soil and pushed dirt out in fan-shaped mounds.

A kaleidoscope created colorful symmetrical patterns by reflecting loose beads between angled mirror strips inside. A periscope allowed viewing above obstacles by reflecting light between two angled mirrors inside a tube. A prism separated white light into a rainbow spectrum by bending each color at different angles. A magnifying glass enlarged the appearance of small objects by bending light through a convex lens.

Terracotta was an unglazed reddish-brown clay used for roof tiles and flower pots worldwide. Porcelain was a fine white translucent ceramic fired at extremely high temperatures in large kilns. Stoneware was a dense opaque ceramic fired at high temperatures and used for durable kitchen crockery. Earthenware was a porous low-fired ceramic often glazed and used for decorative plates and bowls.

A tundra biome had permafrost soil and low scrubby vegetation in cold polar regions. A savanna biome had scattered trees and tall grasses in warm tropical and subtropical regions. A taiga biome had dense coniferous forests and long cold winters in subarctic northern latitudes. A chaparral biome had drought-resistant shrubs and hot dry summers in Mediterranean climate zones.

An astrolabe was used by ancient astronomers to measure the altitude of celestial objects overhead. A quadrant was used by navigators to determine latitude by measuring the angle of the sun. An armillary sphere modeled the celestial coordinate system using interlocking rings around a central earth globe. A planisphere displayed a rotating map of visible stars for any date and time setting.

A dromedary camel had one hump and inhabited the hot dry deserts of North Africa. A guanaco roamed the high dry grasslands and mountains of western South America in herds. A bison grazed on the wide open prairies of the central plains of North America. A water buffalo waded through flooded rice paddies in the tropical lowlands of Southeast Asia.

A portcullis was a heavy grilled gate that dropped vertically to block a castle entrance passage. A moat was a wide water-filled ditch that surrounded a castle wall for defensive protection. A battlement was a low wall with gaps at the top of a castle for defensive fighting. A drawbridge was a hinged wooden platform that raised to block entry across the castle moat.

A haiku was a form of Japanese poetry structured in three short unrhymed measured lines. A ghazal was a form of Arabic and Persian poetry composed of rhyming self-contained couplets. A villanelle was a form of French poetry built on two repeating rhymes in six stanzas. A tanka was a form of Japanese poetry structured in five unrhymed lines of measured syllables.

A mortar and pestle ground dry spices into fine powder using manual crushing force. A rolling pin flattened dough into thin even sheets using back and forth pressure on surfaces. A colander drained liquid from cooked food through many small holes in a bowl shape. A whisk blended liquid ingredients together by rapidly stirring thin wire loops through the mixture.

A geyser erupted periodically by shooting hot water and steam from an underground chamber upward. A fumarole released volcanic gases and steam continuously through a vent in the ground surface. A hot spring pooled heated groundwater at the surface in a calm basin without violent eruption. A mud pot bubbled with hot acidic water mixed with volcanic clay in a shallow depression.

An alligator had a wide rounded snout and lived in freshwater habitats in warm regions. A gharial had a very long narrow snout and lived in rivers on the Indian subcontinent. A caiman had a bony ridge between the eyes and lived in Central and South American waters. A saltwater crocodile had a broad powerful jaw and lived in coastal and estuarine tropical waters.

Cotton canvas was used for painting surfaces and heavy bags and outdoor tent shelters worldwide. Burlap was woven from jute plant fibers and used for sacking and rough packaging material. Denim was a sturdy twill-woven cotton fabric used primarily for durable work trousers and jackets. Corduroy was a ridged cotton fabric with vertical pile strips used for trousers and jackets.

A fresnel lens focused light into a narrow beam using concentric ridged rings on a flat surface. A convex mirror reflected a wide-angle view of the area behind an object for safety. A fiber optic cable transmitted light signals through thin flexible glass strands over long distances quickly. A prism bent white light at different angles and separated the light into rainbow colors.

A chinook wind was a warm dry wind that descended the eastern slopes of the Rocky Mountains. A mistral wind was a cold strong wind that blew southward through the Rhone Valley in France. A sirocco wind was a hot dusty wind that blew northward from the Sahara across the Mediterranean. A harmattan wind was a dry dusty wind that blew westward from the Sahara across West Africa.

A lathe shaped wood or metal by rotating the material against a fixed cutting tool edge. A forge heated metal to a workable temperature using a coal or gas fueled intense fire. An anvil provided a hard flat surface for hammering and shaping heated metal with hand tools. A grinding wheel smoothed and sharpened metal surfaces by spinning an abrasive stone disc rapidly.
